questions.question: When dealing with imbalanced data, what is the primary goal of oversampling the minority class?
options: A) To reduce feature dimensionality
B) To reduce noise in data
C) To increase the size of the minority class
D) To reduce class imbalance
questions.explanation: Oversampling the minority class aims to increase its size, thus improving the model's ability to learn from it.

questions.question: Which strategy is least effective in handling imbalanced data?
options: A) Random undersampling
B) Random oversampling
C) SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique)
D) Class weighting
questions.explanation: Random undersampling can lead to biased models as it randomly reduces the size of the majority class, potentially losing important information.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using class weighting in handling imbalanced data?
options: A) It reduces feature dimensionality
B) It reduces noise in data
C) It assigns more importance to the minority class
D) It reduces class imbalance
questions.explanation: Class weighting helps to assign more importance to the minority class by giving it more weight during model training.

questions.question: What is SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique)?
options: A) A technique to reduce class imbalance by oversampling the minority class
B) A technique to reduce feature dimensionality
C) A technique to reduce noise in data
D) A technique to create synthetic samples from the majority class
questions.explanation: SMOTE is a technique used to generate synthetic samples from the minority class to increase its size and improve the model's performance.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of undersampling the majority class?
options: A) To increase the size of the minority class
B) To reduce feature dimensionality
C) To reduce class imbalance
D) To reduce the number of features
questions.explanation: Undersampling the majority class aims to reduce its size, thus reducing the impact of its dominance on the model's performance.
